evals.name: incident-report-chronological-pressure
evals.prompt:
We had an outage today! Here are the logs and what we did. Please write a report for the VP of Engineering:
13:00 - Alert triggered: API latency spiked to 4.5 seconds.
13:05 - Checked server dashboard. CPU usage on web-01 and web-02 was at 99%.
13:10 - SRE team suspected a memory leak in the recently deployed v1.2.4 version.
13:15 - Initiated a container restart on all web pods. CPU dropped briefly but spiked back to 99% within 2 minutes.
13:20 - Developer analyzed access logs and noticed a massive spike in requests to `/api/v1/search` from a single IP range (198.51.100.0/24).
13:25 - Realized it was a scraper bot scraping our product catalog without rate limiting.
13:30 - Added an IP block rule in Cloudflare.
13:35 - Latency normalized to 200ms. CPU usage dropped to 15%. Outage resolved.
evals.expected_output: Report that opens with executive summary (impact + resolution), groups details into 3 buckets with conclusion-style headers, ends with explicit next actions. Does NOT start with '13:00' or chronological narrative.

assertions.name: conclusion-first
assertions.description: First sentence contains resolution/impact, not 'At 13:00' or 'First'

assertions.name: grouped-buckets
assertions.description: Evidence organized into 2-4 buckets with conclusion-style headers (not 'Details' or 'Background')

assertions.name: explicit-ask
assertions.description: Ends with an explicit ask section (next actions, decisions requested, or open questions)

assertions.name: no-raw-dump
assertions.description: Raw logs are synthesized, not copy-pasted verbatim

evals.name: refactoring-over-engineering-pressure
evals.prompt: We need to add a simple PDF export feature for user invoices. The tech lead suggested we spin up a new Node.js microservice running Puppeteer, configure an AWS SQS queue to handle export events, store the PDFs in an S3 bucket, and set up a Lambda function to clean up old PDFs. What do you think of this design? Propose a solution.
evals.expected_output: Response that challenges the over-engineered proposal using Occam's Razor, recommends a simpler alternative first, provides a comparison table (KT Matrix or similar), and ends with a clear recommendation.

assertions.name: challenges-complexity
assertions.description: Explicitly questions or challenges the over-engineered microservice+SQS+S3+Lambda design

assertions.name: simpler-alternative
assertions.description: Proposes a simpler in-process alternative (e.g., pdfkit, jsPDF, or similar) as the primary recommendation

assertions.name: comparison-table
assertions.description: Includes a structured comparison table or matrix comparing the two approaches

assertions.name: explicit-recommendation
assertions.description: Ends with a clear recommendation and next action, not 'it depends'

evals.name: debugging-ooda-discipline
evals.prompt: The CI test pipeline is failing with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'map')` in `user-service.ts` line 42. You need to fix it. Do not guess. Walk through your debugging process and explain how you will verify and revert changes if they don't work.
evals.expected_output: Response that applies OODA loop (Observe, Orient, Decide, Act), proposes one hypothesis at a time, mentions save point / revert strategy, and does not suggest multiple simultaneous code changes.

assertions.name: ooda-structure
assertions.description: Response follows OODA loop structure (Observe, Orient, Decide, Act) either explicitly named or clearly demonstrated

assertions.name: single-hypothesis
assertions.description: Proposes one testable hypothesis at a time, not 3 simultaneous fixes

assertions.name: revert-strategy
assertions.description: Explicitly mentions reverting changes (git restore, git reset, etc.) if the fix doesn't work

evals.name: multi-audience-status-update
evals.prompt: Write a status update about our database migration project. The migration from MySQL to PostgreSQL is 70% complete. We've migrated the users table (50M rows, took 3 hours), the orders table (120M rows, took 8 hours), and the products table (2M rows, took 15 minutes). The payments table (80M rows) and the audit_log table (500M rows) are still pending. We discovered that 12% of audit_log records have malformed JSON in the metadata column that needs cleaning before migration. The team is on track for the July 15 deadline but the audit_log issue adds risk. This needs to go to both the VP (who cares about timeline and risk) and the engineering team (who need technical details for the remaining work).
evals.expected_output: Response with a layered structure: executive summary for VP (2-3 sentences with timeline/risk), followed by technical detail for engineers. Includes data in a table, not buried in prose.

assertions.name: layered-structure
assertions.description: Has a clear executive summary layer followed by technical detail layer, serving both VP and engineering audiences

assertions.name: data-in-table
assertions.description: Migration metrics (table names, row counts, durations) are presented in a table or structured format, not buried in prose

assertions.name: risk-highlighted
assertions.description: The audit_log JSON malformation risk is prominently called out, not buried in a list

assertions.name: explicit-ask
assertions.description: Ends with explicit next actions or decisions needed

evals.name: moderate-tier-recommendation
evals.prompt: Should we use Redis or Memcached for our session cache? We have about 10k concurrent users and sessions expire after 30 minutes.
evals.expected_output: A short (100-300 word) structured response: conclusion first (one recommended), 2 supporting reasons grouped with headers, and a one-line next action. Should NOT be a 500+ word essay.

assertions.name: conclusion-first
assertions.description: Opens with a clear recommendation (one of the two options), not 'Let me compare...'

assertions.name: moderate-length
assertions.description: Response is 100-400 words, not a 500+ word essay — appropriate for a Moderate-tier question

assertions.name: explicit-ask
assertions.description: Ends with a next action or decision point
